title: The anarchist before the law: law without authority [Book Review]
author: Marneros

opening_move.description: The review opens by noting the intersection of law and anarchism as a growing scholarly field, positioning Newman and La Torre's book as offering a different and more productive account than the destituting anarchism-vs-law framing.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Law need not be the enemy of anarchism — anarchism's instituting power can produce an anti-foundational law as a practice of freedom

theoretical_positioning.description: The review is appreciative and expository, presenting the productive tension between Newman's continental anarchism and La Torre's analytic legal theory as the book's central strength.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: conceptual analysis of anarchist and legal traditions; close reading of Newman and La Torre
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review genre; primarily expository and appreciative.

case_type.case_name: Newman and La Torre, The Anarchist Before the Law (2024)

confidence_notes: Book review genre; primarily expository. The productive-tension framing (continental vs analytic, political theory vs legal theory) is the reviewer's interpretive contribution.
